whatThisIs: Search the full CMS HCPCS Level II modifier file - the two-character alpha and alphanumeric suffixes that ride along on HCPCS codes to refine site, payer logic, or patient circumstance. Sophie Well covers the laterality pair (LT, RT), the ABN family (GA, GX, GY, GZ), the ambulance origin-and-destination single-character pairings (e.g., RH, HD), the catastrophe disaster modifier CR, and every other active Level II two-character code with the official CMS short description.
whenToUse: Use this when a claim line denies for a missing or wrong HCPCS modifier, when an ambulance trip needs an origin/destination pair built up letter by letter, or when a clinician asks which ABN modifier signals patient acknowledgment versus statutory exclusion. Level II modifiers carry payment and audit consequences distinct from CPT modifiers - the GY/GZ distinction alone determines whether the claim is denied as not-covered or denied as not-medically-necessary - so verifying the exact letter pair before submission is reference work that prevents a re-bill cycle later.
